overview: The witest28-6 system is a single-component, lightweight Hello World web application built as a Go HTTP server. It serves a static HTML page at the root path, exposes a /health endpoint for liveness checks, and is packaged as a Docker container deployable to Kubernetes. There are no inter-service dependencies — all routing, security headers, and access logging are handled within the single service.
requirements: The application must serve a web page at the root path (/) displaying a visible 'Hello, World!' greeting as primary content.
The page must have a valid HTML <title> element (e.g., 'Hello World').
The application must return HTTP 200 OK for successful page loads at /.
Any undefined route must return a user-friendly HTTP 404 Page Not Found response.
The application must expose a /health endpoint returning HTTP 200 with JSON body {"status": "ok"}.
The listening port must be configurable via the PORT environment variable, defaulting to 8080.
The application must emit access logs (timestamp, request path, HTTP status code) to stdout.
The application must include HTTP security headers: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, and must not expose server/framework version info.
The HTML page must be valid, well-formed, responsive (no horizontal scrolling on mobile), and meet WCAG 2.1 Level AA color contrast and text structure standards.
The total page weight must not exceed 500 KB.
The page must fully load within 2 seconds on a standard broadband connection (≥10 Mbps).
The application must handle at least 100 concurrent users without degraded response times.
The application must recover automatically from crashes or restarts without manual intervention.
In production environments, the application must be served over HTTPS.

components.name: hello-world-server
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: witest28-6 Hello World API
  description: Minimal Hello World web application serving an HTML page and health endpoint.
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /:
    get:
      summary: Hello World page
      description: Returns the Hello World HTML page with HTTP 200.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: HTML page with Hello World greeting.
          headers:
            Content-Security-Policy:
              schema:
                type: string
            X-Frame-Options:
              schema:
                type: string
            X-Content-Type-Options:
              schema:
                type: string
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      description: Returns service health status.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /{path}:
    get:
      summary: Catch-all 404
      description: Returns a user-friendly 404 page for any undefined route.
      parameters:
        - name: path
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '404':
          description: Page not found.
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
components.componentAgentInstructions:
## Component: hello-world-server ### Language & Framework - **Language:** Go (1.22+) - **HTTP library:** Standard library `net/http` only — no third-party frameworks. - **Port:** Configurable via `PORT` environment variable; default `8080`. ### Project Structure ``` /hello-world-server main.go Dockerfile go.mod ``` ### go.mod - Module name: `github.com/witest28-6/hello-world-server` - Go version: `1.22` - No external dependencies. ### Dockerfile - Multi-stage build. - Stage 1: `golang:1.22-alpine` — build static binary (`CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux`). - Stage 2: `scratch` or `gcr.io/distroless/static` — copy binary only. - Expose port `8080` (the default). The actual port at runtime is governed by the `PORT` env var. - `CMD ["/server"]` ### Routing (main.go) Register exactly three routes on a `http.ServeMux`: 1. **`GET /`** → `rootHandler` 2. **`GET /health`** → `healthHandler` 3. **`GET /` catch-all (any other path)** → handled inside `rootHandler` by checking `r.URL.Path != "/"` and writing a 404 response. Alternatively use a single handler on `"/"` (which is the default catch-all in Go's ServeMux) and branch inside: - If `r.URL.Path == "/"` → serve HTML page. - Else → serve 404 HTML page. Register `/health` separately so it takes priority. ### Security Middleware Wrap all handlers with a middleware function that sets these response headers **before** writing any body: ``` Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self' X-Frame-Options: DENY X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff Referrer-Policy: no-referrer Server: (empty string or remove entirely) ``` To suppress the `Server` header, set it to an empty string after calling the underlying handler, or use a `ResponseWriter` wrapper. ### Access Logging Middleware Wrap handlers with a logging middleware that writes to stdout in this format: ``` 2026-04-14T10:00:00Z GET / 200 ``` Fields: ISO 8601 timestamp, HTTP method, request path, response status code. Use a `responseWriterWrapper` struct that captures the status code written by the downstream handler. ### rootHandler — HTML Response Write `Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8` and status `200 OK`. Inline the HTML as a Go string constant: ```html Hello World

Hello, World!

Welcome to witest28-6.

``` Design notes: - Color contrast ratio of `#1a1a1a` on `#ffffff` is well above 7:1 (WCAG AAA), satisfying AA. - `
` landmark element for accessibility. - `lang="en"` on `` for screen readers. - Responsive via `clamp()` font size and no fixed widths. - No external resources — zero network round-trips beyond the page itself (well under 500 KB). ### 404 Handler When `r.URL.Path != "/"` write `Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8` and status `404 Not Found`. Return a minimal HTML page: ```html Page Not Found

404 – Page Not Found

Go to Home

``` ### healthHandler - Write `Content-Type: application/json` and status `200 OK`. - Body: `{"status":"ok"}` - No logging middleware wrapping needed, but it must still go through security headers middleware. ### Startup ```go port := os.Getenv("PORT") if port == "" { port = "8080" } log.Printf("Starting server on port %s", port) if err := http.ListenAndServe(":"+port, mux); err != nil { log.Fatalf("Server failed: %v", err) } ``` ### Concurrency & Performance - Go's `net/http` server is concurrent by default (goroutine per connection); no additional configuration needed to handle 100+ concurrent users for a static HTML response. - Do NOT read/write to any shared mutable state in handlers (the HTML is a compile-time constant). ### Edge Cases - `PORT` env var set to non-numeric string: the `ListenAndServe` call will fail with a clear error logged to stdout — acceptable for this scope. - Method other than GET on `/` or `/health`: Go's default mux does not filter by method; respond normally (no body needed for HEAD; POST will still get the HTML — acceptable for this minimal scope). - Trailing slash on non-root paths (e.g., `/foo/`): caught by the catch-all and returns 404. ### No Database, No External Calls This service is entirely self-contained. Do not add any database, cache, or external HTTP client code.
